title: Europe Rejects Trump’s Demands for Warships to Reopen the Strait of Hormuz
summary: While some European countries said they were discussing ways to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, several rejected President Trump’s calls to send warships.

title: What Are Trump’s Positions on Iran? They Can Change by the Sentence.
summary: The president is no stranger to staking out contradictory stands, part of what his aides say is his negotiating style. But on Iran, his shifting positions are colliding with the consequences of war.

title: What the Iran War Means for the U.S.-China Relationship
summary: With the Xi-Trump summit almost certainly delayed, and tensions rising over the war in Iran, vital issues for both the U.S. and China are also being cast into uncertainty.

title: As American Bombers Take Off From R.A.F. Fairford in UK, Iraq War Looms Large
summary: R.A.F. Fairford was the site of repeated antiwar protests during the Iraq war in 2003. Now it is being used again as a base for U.S. bombing missions in the Middle East.

title: Cuba Ready to Accept Outside Investment, Top Official Says
summary: President Trump’s words came amid a nationwide blackout and as a top Cuban official said his country would move to open the economy to foreign investors.

title: Trump Administration Seeks to Remove Cuba’s President From Power During Negotiations
summary: The United States has told Cuba that for meaningful progress to be made in negotiations, President Miguel Díaz-Canel must step down, said people familiar with the talks.

title: Oil Begins Flowing Through California Pipeline Under Trump Order
summary: Oil had not flowed through the pipeline since a 2015 rupture caused an environmental disaster on California’s Central Coast. It sets up a new fight between the Trump administration and state officials.

title: War Isn’t the Only Thing on Trump’s Mind
summary: President Trump spoke to journalists for about 40 minutes before holding a news conference on Monday, offering a glimpse into his priorities.

title: Judge Strikes Down RFK Jr.’s Vaccine Policies in Blow to Trump’s Health Agenda
summary: Ruling on a lawsuit brought by several prominent medical organizations, a district court said the federal government had not based its decisions on science in limiting Covid shots and revising the childhood immunization schedule.

title: Airport Security Lines Grow as TSA Goes Unpaid in Partial Shutdown
summary: “There’s going to be a breaking point sooner or later,” one union official warned, with travelers at some airports being told to arrive three hours ahead of time.

title: Richard Grenell: From Trump Cabinet Hopeful to Ex-Kennedy Center Manager
summary: Richard Grenell once hoped to be President Trump’s secretary of state. Instead, Mr. Trump just replaced him as Kennedy Center president.

title: Best and Worst Moments From the 2026 Oscars
summary: There was a lot to take in, from Michael B. Jordan’s thrilling win to the perplexing “bum drum.”

title: How ‘Marty Supreme’ and Timothée Chalamet Went Home With No Oscars
summary: A few years ago, the indie studio A24 was luxuriating in Academy Awards. On Sunday it was shut out entirely.

title: For Once, the Oscars Got a Lot Right (Even the In Memoriam Segment)
summary: The ceremony figured out how to celebrate movies and the people who make them. It even understood Robert Redford’s place in American cinema.

title: Dinner and No Drinks: Restaurants Are Struggling as Americans Drink Less
summary: Traditionally a reliable revenue stream for restaurants, alcoholic drinks are down markedly — and the bottom line is, too.

title: In Deep-Red Idaho, a Republican Rift Over Schools and ‘Parental Choice’
summary: Does “choice” in Idaho mean vouchers for private-school tuition or publicly funded remote learning that has brought AP classes and advanced math to the state’s rural reaches?

title: Kouri Richins Found Guilty of Poisoning Her Husband in Utah Murder Trial
summary: Kouri Richins was accused of mixing a lethal dose of fentanyl into a drink she made for her husband. She later wrote a children’s book about coping with grief.

title: Is Cheburashka, the Beloved Soviet-Born Character, Ruining Russia?
summary: Instead of obsessing over the fictional Cheburashka, Russians should be focused on more important things like the rebirth of a Russian empire, influential conservatives say.

title: Trump’s Dangerous Lack of a Strategy in Iran
summary: The early reality of the Iran war is not cooperating with President Trump’s bluster.

title: How Trump Destroyed the Aura of the Wartime President
summary: Even as he’s wrecking American institutions, he is revealing the limits of his cultural influence.

title: Jimmy Kimmel Has a Bone to Pick With Trump’s War Plans
summary: “The only war Trump had an exit plan for was Vietnam,” Kimmel remarked after the president said he would end the war in Iran when he “feels it in his bones.”

title: University of Florida Penalizes GOP Group Over Antisemitism Accusations
summary: After the University of Florida restricted the Republican organization from operating on campus, the group sued the university arguing its First Amendment rights were violated.

title: Colorado Funeral Home Owner Is Sentenced to 18 Years on Federal Fraud Charges
summary: Carie Hallford, 49, and her husband failed to provide cremation and burial services they had promised to grieving families, prosecutors said.

title: Judy Pace, 83, Dies; Actress Brought Layers to Black Characters
summary: On the prime-time soap opera “Peyton Place,” she played one of TV’s first Black female antagonists. She was also a fixture in blaxploitation films.

title: 4 Are Dead, Including a Child, in a Fast-Moving Queens Fire
summary: The building in Flushing was engulfed in flames by the time firefighters arrived four minutes after a 911 call. Twelve people were injured, at least one critically.
